Class ARGB

java.lang.Object
net.fokinatorr.sparkle.graphics.ARGB
public class ARGB extends Object
Utility methods that help treating an int value as a ARGB-format color.

ARGB stands for Alpha (opacity), Red, Green, Blue. Each of these properties are called color components or channels. Each component takes up 8 bits of the number, which means they range between 0 and 255 inclusive, and the resulting component values in blending methods are clamped between that range.

The bits of an int value are treated as follows, assuming bit 0 is the most significant bit, and bit 31 is the least significant bit:

  • bits from 0 to 7 representing alpha value,
  • bits from 8 to 15 representing red value,
  • bits from 16 to 23 representing green value,
  • bits from 24 to 31 representing blue value.

Note for Scratchers: Subtractive and multiplicative blendings have 2 different variations: layered (rgbXxx()) and true (blendXxx()). Layered variants always use additive blending for alpha channels - similarly to what Scratch does when half-transparent sprites overlap. True variants use the same blending for all channels, which is what most modern software (including image editors) do. Be careful with true blendings; for example, using true subtractive blending on two opaque colors will result in a fully transparent color!

    Sets the alpha value for the given color.

    Multiplies the given two colors' components.

    Subtracts the second color's components from the first color's components.

      public byte alpha(int color)
      Returns the alpha value of the given color (unsigned).
      Parameters:
      color - the color
      Returns:
      the alpha value

    • red

      public byte red(int color)
      Returns the red value of the given color (unsigned).
      Parameters:
      color - the color
      Returns:
      the red value

    • green

      public byte green(int color)
      Returns the green value of the given color (unsigned).
      Parameters:
      color - the color
      Returns:
      the green value

    • blue

      public byte blue(int color)
      Returns the blue value of the given color (unsigned).
      Parameters:
      color - the color
      Returns:
      the blue value

    • alpha

      public int alpha(int color, byte alpha)
      Sets the alpha value for the given color.
      Parameters:
      color - the color
      alpha - the new alpha value
      Returns:
      the new color

    • red

      public int red(int color, byte red)
      Sets the red value for the given color.
      Parameters:
      color - the color
      red - the new red value
      Returns:
      the new color

    • green

      public int green(int color, byte green)
      Sets the green value for the given color.
      Parameters:
      color - the color
      green - the new green value
      Returns:
      the new color

    • blue

      public int blue(int color, byte blue)
      Sets the blue value for the given color.
      Parameters:
      color - the color
      blue - the new blue value
      Returns:
      the new color

    • color

      public int color(byte alpha, byte red, byte green, byte blue)
      Creates a new color with the given values.
      Parameters:
      alpha - the alpha value
      red - the red value
      green - the green value
      blue - the blue value
      Returns:
      a new color

    • opaqueColor

      public int opaqueColor(byte red, byte green, byte blue)
      Creates a new, fully opaque color with the given values.
      Parameters:
      red - the red value
      green - the green value
      blue - the blue value
      Returns:
      a new color

    • transparentColor

      public int transparentColor(byte red, byte green, byte blue)
      Creates a new, fully transparent color with the given values. No matter which values were passed, the color will not be visible until its alpha value becomes greater than 0.
      Parameters:
      red - the red value
      green - the green value
      blue - the blue value
      Returns:
      a new color

    • toString

      public String toString(int color)
      Returns a hexadecimal color notation string representation of the given color. That is, 
      #AARRGGBB
      where each letter pair represents one component value in hexadecimal notation.
      Parameters:
      color - the color
      Returns:
      the color's string representation

    • add

      public int add(int color1, int color2)
      Sums the given two colors' components. The layered additive blending and true additive blending do the same thing, so this method is responsible for both of these blending variants.
      Parameters:
      color1 - the first color
      color2 - the second color
      Returns:
      the additive blending of the given colors

    • rgbSubtract

      public int rgbSubtract(int color1, int color2)
      Subtracts the second color's RGB components from the first color's RGB components. The alpha channels get summed instead, resulting in a layered blending.
      Parameters:
      color1 - the first color
      color2 - the second color
      Returns:
      the layered subtractive blending of the given colors

    • blendSubtract

      public int blendSubtract(int color1, int color2)
      Subtracts the second color's components from the first color's components. The alpha channels also get subtracted, resulting in a true blending.
      Parameters:
      color1 - the first color
      color2 - the second color
      Returns:
      the true subtractive blending of the given colors

    • rgbMultiply

      public int rgbMultiply(int color1, int color2)
      Multiplies the given two colors' RGB components. The alpha channels get summed instead, resulting in a layered blending.
      Parameters:
      color1 - the first color
      color2 - the second color
      Returns:
      the layered multiplicative blending of the given colors

    • blendMultiply

      public int blendMultiply(int color1, int color2)
      Multiplies the given two colors' components. The alpha channels also get multiplied, resulting in a true blending.
      Parameters:
      color1 - the first color
      color2 - the second color
      Returns:
      the true multiplicative blending of the given colors

    • opaque

      public int opaque(int color)
      Turns the given color into its fully opaque version.
      Parameters:
      color - the color
      Returns:
      the new, fully opaque color

    • transparent

      public int transparent(int color)
      Turns the given color into its fully transparent version. The color will not be visible until its alpha value becomes more than 0.
      Parameters:
      color - the color
      Returns:
      the new, fully transparent color
